question_claim.claim: Under existing Department of Health Care Services (DHCS) contract requirements and state law, Managed Care Plans (MCPs) are required to provide hospice services upon Managed Care Plan Member election to start and receive hospice care services.
question_claim.source_text: under existing Contract requirements and state law, MCPs are required to provide hospice services upon Member election to start and receive such care services

policy_claim.claim: CalOptima Health shall ensure Managed Care Plan Members receive Hospice Care upon election, in accordance with DHCS contract requirements and state law.
policy_claim.source_text: CalOptima Health and its Health Networks shall be responsible for ensuring the provision of Hospice Care services for Terminally Ill Members

best_match.section: II.A Hospice Coverage and Member Election
best_match.rationale: Section II.A restates the requirement verbatim: MCPs must ensure hospice coverage upon Member election, citing DHCS contract authority.
best_match.policy_title: Hospice Coverage for Medi-Cal Managed Care Plan Members

question_claim.claim: Hospice coverage for Managed Care Plan Members is provided in two 90-day benefit periods, beginning on the date of hospice election.
question_claim.source_text: Hospice coverage is provided in benefit periods: Two 90-day periods, beginning on the date of hospice election

policy_claim.claim: Hospice coverage for Managed Care Plan Members is provided in two 90-day benefit periods, beginning on the date of hospice election.
policy_claim.source_text: A Member may elect to receive hospice benefits for two (2) election periods consisting of ninety (90) calendar days each

best_match.rationale: Section II.B mirrors the regulator's structure: two 90-day periods keyed to the date of hospice election.
best_match.policy_title: Hospice Coverage for Medi-Cal Managed Care Plan Members

question_claim.claim: The initial two 90-day hospice benefit periods for Managed Care Plan Members are followed by unlimited 60-day periods.

policy_claim.claim: After the initial two 90-day benefit periods, Managed Care Plan Members are entitled to unlimited 60-day hospice benefit periods.
policy_claim.source_text: an unlimited number of subsequent periods of sixty (60) calendar days each

best_match.rationale: Section II.B explicitly extends coverage into unlimited 60-day periods after the initial two.
best_match.policy_title: Hospice Coverage for Medi-Cal Managed Care Plan Members

question_claim.claim: A hospice benefit period starts the day the Managed Care Plan (MCP) Member receives hospice care and ends when the 90-day or 60-day period ends.
question_claim.source_text: A benefit period starts the day the Member receives hospice care and ends when the 90-day or 60-day period ends.

policy_claim.claim: Each hospice benefit period starts the day the Managed Care Plan Member first receives hospice care and ends when the corresponding 90-day or 60-day period elapses.
policy_claim.source_text: At any time during an election period, a Member may elect to revoke or modify Hospice Care.

best_match.rationale: Section II.B defines the benefit-period window exactly as the regulator requires — day of first hospice care to elapse of the 90/60-day clock.
best_match.policy_title: Hospice Coverage for Medi-Cal Managed Care Plan Members

question_claim.claim: Managed Care Plan (MCP) Members who qualify for and elect to receive hospice care services remain enrolled in an MCP while receiving hospice care services.
question_claim.source_text: Members who qualify for and elect to receive hospice care services remain enrolled in an MCP while receiving such services

policy_claim.claim: Managed Care Plan Members who elect hospice care remain enrolled in the MCP and continue to receive all non-terminal-illness benefits through the plan.
policy_claim.source_text: CalOptima Health and its Health Networks, in accordance with the Health Network-specific DOFR, are responsible for the provision of all Medically Necessary Covered Services not related to a Member’s Terminal Illness

best_match.section: II.A Hospice Coverage and Member Election
best_match.rationale: Section II.A guarantees members who elect hospice stay enrolled and keep non-terminal-illness benefits through the plan, matching the questionnaire's enrollment-continuity ask.
best_match.policy_title: Hospice Coverage for Medi-Cal Managed Care Plan Members

question_claim.claim: Managed Care Plan (MCP) Policies and Procedures (P&Ps) should clarify and detail how MCP Members may access hospice care services in a timely manner, preferably within 24 hours of the request from in-Network hospice Providers.
question_claim.source_text: MCP P&Ps should clarify and detail how Members may access hospice care services in a timely manner, preferably within 24 hours of the request from in-Network hospice Providers

policy_claim.claim: CalOptima Health shall ensure Managed Care Plan Members are referred to hospice services in a timely manner, with a target of within 24 hours of request from in-Network hospice Providers.
policy_claim.source_text: Medi-Cal primary Members may access Hospice Care within twenty-four (24) hours of Notification of Hospice request.

best_match.rationale: Section III.A carries the 24-hour timely-access target for in-Network hospice referrals — exact numeric match on the APL requirement.
best_match.policy_title: Hospice Coverage for Medi-Cal Managed Care Plan Members

question_claim.claim: Managed Care Plans (MCPs) may restrict hospice coverage to in-Network Providers, unless Medically Necessary services are not available in-Network, consistent with contractual requirements for covered Medi-Cal benefits.
question_claim.source_text: MCPs may restrict coverage to in-Network Providers, unless Medically Necessary services are not available in-Network, consistent with contractual requirements for covered Medi-Cal benefits

policy_claim.claim: Managed Care Plans may restrict hospice coverage to in-Network Providers, unless Medically Necessary services are not available in-Network under Medi-Cal benefits.
policy_claim.source_text: CalOptima Health or its Health Networks, in accordance with the Health Network-specific DOFR, shall be responsible for providing Hospice Care to a Medi-Cal primary Member

best_match.rationale: Section III.B adopts the regulator's permit-and-exception structure: restrict to in-Network unless Medically Necessary services are not available.
best_match.policy_title: Hospice Coverage for Medi-Cal Managed Care Plan Members

question_claim.claim: Managed Care Plan (MCP) Members who elect hospice care are entitled to curative treatment for conditions unrelated to their terminal illness.
question_claim.source_text: Members who elect hospice care are entitled to curative treatment for conditions unrelated to their terminal illness

question_claim.claim: For out-of-Network hospice Providers, the Managed Care Plan (MCP) should seek an agreement, such as a single case agreement or a letter of agreement, to cover hospice care services.
question_claim.source_text: for out-of-Network hospice Providers, the MCP should seek an agreement, such as a single case agreement or a letter of agreement, to cover hospice care services

policy_claim.claim: For out-of-Network hospice Providers, the Managed Care Plan shall seek an agreement — such as a Single Case Agreement or Letter of Agreement — to ensure coverage for the Managed Care Plan Member.
policy_claim.source_text: A Hospice Provider shall be responsible for services related to the Member’s Terminal Illness, including, but not limited to, pharmacy, physician, social services, nursing, home health aide services

best_match.rationale: Section IV.A names Single Case Agreement and Letter of Agreement verbatim as the mechanisms for out-of-Network hospice coverage.
best_match.policy_title: Hospice Coverage for Medi-Cal Managed Care Plan Members

question_claim.claim: Agreements between Managed Care Plans (MCPs) and out-of-Network hospice Providers require the Provider to submit necessary documentation for the MCP to ensure hospice services are provided in accordance with coverage policy and Medical Necessity.
question_claim.source_text: agreements with the out-of-Network hospice Provider require the Provider to submit necessary documentation for the MCP to ensure that hospice services are provided in accordance with coverage policy, including Medical Necessity

policy_claim.claim: Agreements between Managed Care Plans and out-of-Network hospice Providers require the Provider to follow the MCP's documentation and Medical Necessity coverage policies.
policy_claim.source_text: If a hospice provider does not provide adequate clinical documentation or documents do not substantiate the need for General Inpatient Care, the CalOptima Health Medical Director may reduce the Level of Care to Routine Home Care.

best_match.rationale: Section IV.A requires out-of-Network hospice Providers to follow MCP documentation and Medical Necessity coverage policies, matching the regulator's documentation ask.
best_match.policy_title: Hospice Coverage for Medi-Cal Managed Care Plan Members

question_claim.claim: Prior Authorization by Managed Care Plans (MCPs) for hospice services is restricted based on the level of care.
question_claim.source_text: Prior Authorization for hospice services is restricted, based on the level of care

policy_claim.claim: Prior Authorization is required by the Managed Care Plan for all hospice services regardless of level of care or benefit period.
policy_claim.source_text: CalOptima Health shall not require prior authorization for Hospice Care services under Routine Home Care, Continuous Home Care, and Respite Care levels of Hospice Care.

contradictions.section: V.A Prior Authorization and Utilization Review
contradictions.rationale: Section V.A flatly requires Prior Authorization for ALL hospice services regardless of level of care — directly contradicts APL 25-008's limit on PA. Surface this as a compliance risk.
contradictions.policy_title: Hospice Coverage for Medi-Cal Managed Care Plan Members

question_claim.claim: Managed Care Plans (MCPs) are required to review hospice documentation to avoid Fraud, Waste, and Abuse.
question_claim.source_text: MCPs are required to review documentation to avoid Fraud, Waste, and Abuse

policy_claim.claim: Managed Care Plans shall review hospice documentation to detect and prevent Fraud, Waste, and Abuse.
policy_claim.source_text: A Hospice Provider shall submit Notification of Hospice election immediately and no later than thirty (30) calendar days after start of services.

best_match.section: V.A Prior Authorization and Utilization Review
best_match.rationale: Section V.A obligates MCPs to review hospice documentation to detect Fraud, Waste, and Abuse — exact coverage of the regulator's FWA review ask.
best_match.policy_title: Hospice Coverage for Medi-Cal Managed Care Plan Members
